mechanism_narrative: Persistent high federal funds rates increase the cost of capital for corporations, leading to lower investment and reduced earnings expectations, which negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.33% in t-1mo is historically high
failure_condition: If S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are consistently positive during periods of high FEDFUNDS, or if the correlation between FEDFUNDS and returns becomes positive over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant rise in the unemployment rate signals a weakening labor market, which typically precedes a slowdown in consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.8% in t-1mo is still low but a sharp rise would be a trigger
failure_condition: If a rise in UNRATE by more than 0.5pp over 3 months is followed by positive S&P 500 60-day returns in more than 50% of occurrences over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales combined with robust housing starts indicate healthy consumer demand and investment, which drives corporate revenue growth and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month average growth > 0.5% AND HOUST 3-month average growth > 0.5%
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 697557.0 and HOUST at 1283.0 in t-2mo show current demand levels
failure_condition: If periods where both RSAFS and HOUST show strong growth are followed by negative S&P 500 20-day returns more than 40% of the time over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating inflation, as measured by CPIAUCSL, erodes purchasing power and corporate margins, forcing the Federal Reserve to maintain a hawkish stance, which negatively impacts equity valuations in a high inflation regime over a 5-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 307.481 in t-1mo indicates elevated inflation
failure_condition: If S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are positive during periods when CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth consistently exceeds 3.0% for more than 3 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP indicates a healthy and expanding economy, which translates into higher corporate earnings and investor confidence, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 22225.35 in t-6mo shows recent economic output
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 consistently delivers negative 60-day returns during periods of positive GDPC1 growth, or if the correlation between GDPC1 growth and returns turns negative over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Robust non-farm payroll employment growth signifies a strong labor market, leading to higher consumer confidence and spending, which positively impacts corporate revenues and S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average growth > 100,000 persons per month
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 156874.0 in t-1mo indicates strong employment
failure_condition: If periods of strong PAYEMS growth (above 100k/month average) are followed by negative S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 35% of cases over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in industrial production reflects strong manufacturing and mining output, indicating healthy supply-side capacity and demand for goods, which supports corporate profitability and S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 103.4886 in t-2mo shows current industrial activity
failure_condition: If S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are consistently negative during periods of positive INDPRO growth, or if the correlation between INDPRO growth and returns becomes negative over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Reserve aggressively raises interest rates (FEDFUNDS) in response to high and persistent inflation (CPIAUCSL), it signals a strong commitment to disinflation, which can lead to a significant slowdown in economic activity and negatively impact S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by > 0.75% in 3 months AND CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth > 4.0%
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.33% and CPIAUCSL at 307.481 in t-1mo indicate current high rates and inflation
regime_hint: aggressive monetary tightening against high inflation
failure_condition: If S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive during periods where FEDFUNDS rises sharply while CPIAUCSL remains elevated, or if the market rallies despite these conditions in more than 30% of triggered instances.
